2.speaker: Crab
2.text: A dinosaur? I suppose that's what you could call it.

3.speaker: Crab
3.text: There have been many big things that come and go.

5.speaker: Crab
5.text: The world was ruled by creatures this big... but now? They're no more...

8.speaker: Crab
8.text: And why is that? It feels meaningless, no? Being the mightiest of all, just... to end up as nothing again.

8.2.speaker: Crab
8.2.text: And why is that? It feels meaningless, no? Being the mightiest of all, just... to end up as nothing again.

choices.text: That's just life. Even if we're here only briefly, that doesn't mean we don't matter.

10.speaker: Crab
10.text: I've been here for so long and am insignificant.

10.2.speaker: Crab
10.2.text: I've been here for so long and am insignificant.

choices.text: The dinosaurs were only here briefly, but to them, their ruling was for their own eternity.

10.3.speaker: Crab
10.3.text: ...Then it wasn’t the time that mattered, was it?

11.speaker: You
11.text: Time was never the source of their meaning in the first place. And it doesn't have to be yours.

13.speaker: Crab
13.text: I shall dwell on it. Thank you.
